title: Nematocyst Tongue
definition: A specialized feeding surface on the tongue of a vampire bat, covered in microscopic spring-loaded cellular structures that penetrate the host's skin to access blood vessels.

difficulty_levels.beginner: Think of a tongue covered in tiny needles that can shoot out and pierce your skin to draw blood. This is similar to the structure found on the tongue of a vampire bat.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: The tongue of a vampire bat is not a smooth muscular organ but a specialized feeding surface covered in tens of thousands of nematocysts, which are microscopic, spring-loaded cellular structures that penetrate the host's skin to access blood vessels.
difficulty_levels.expert: The structure of the vampire bat's tongue involves microscopic, spring-loaded cellular structures called nematocysts that discharge on contact with skin, penetrating into capillary beds. This is a unique adaptation for hematophagy (blood-feeding), allowing the bat to feed silently and efficiently.

extra_fields.insight: A specialized tongue structure for silent and efficient blood-feeding
